channel.title: AI News Digest by Alex Chen (English)
channel.description: Daily AI industry news digest covering Anthropic, OpenAI, and other major players, with in-depth analysis from veteran tech journalist Alex Chen.

item.title: AI Industry Daily - April 22, 2026
item.description: OpenAI says Codex has passed 4 million weekly active developers and is partnering with global systems integrators to push enterprise deployment, marking a shift from personal coding assistance to organizational execution. Anthropic keeps advancing automated alignment research, Google is betting on both open models and controllable voice interfaces, and NVIDIA is building the runtime, security, and inference story for enterprise agents.

item.title: AI Industry Daily - April 16, 2026
item.description: OpenAI added native harness, sandbox, memory, and workspace abstractions to the Agents SDK, pushing agent infrastructure toward standardization. Anthropic's AAR research showed models may accelerate parts of alignment work. Google kept strengthening Gemma 4, while NVIDIA reframed infrastructure competition around cost per token.

item.title: AI Industry Daily - April 15, 2026
item.description: Anthropic used nine Claude copies to automate alignment research, pushing PGR from 0.23 to 0.97. OpenAI expanded Trusted Access for Cyber and launched GPT-5.4-Cyber. Google used AI Impact Summit to deepen infrastructure, science, and government partnerships.

item.title: AI Industry Daily - April 7, 2026
item.description: OpenAI closes $122B at $852B valuation. Anthropic discovers functional "emotions" in Claude that causally drive behavior. Google releases Gemma 4 under Apache 2.0. Money, safety, openness, the three axes of AI competition all move forward.

item.title: AI Industry Daily - March 30, 2026
item.description: OpenAI's Model Spec explained. Safety Bug Bounty launched. Anthropic's Economic Index shows experienced users unlock more value. DeepMind proposes cognitive framework for measuring AGI.
